title: CBDT Revises Monetary Limits for Income Tax Jurisdiction in Delhi Region
content:
# CBDT Revises Monetary Limits for Income Tax Jurisdiction in Delhi Region

Ek important update hai! Income Tax Department ne Delhi Region mein wards aur circles ke jurisdictional monetary limits revise kiye hain. Isse tax assessment process mein kafi clarity aayegi aur case allocation bhi streamline hoga.

Office of the Principal Chief Commissioner of Income Tax (CCA), Delhi, ne Office Order F.No. Addl.CIT(HQ)(Coord.)/Revision of income limites/2026-27/1701 dated May 15, 2026, ke through yeh changes notify kiye hain.

Yahan main points hain:

*   **Purane Limits (as per Instruction No. 01/2011, Metro Cities):**
    *   **Corporate Returns:** Income Tax Officers (ITOs) ke paas ₹30 Lakhs tak ke cases the, aur Deputy Commissioners/Assistant Commissioners (DCs/ACs) ₹30 Lakhs se upar ke cases handle karte the.
    *   **Non-Corporate Returns:** ITOs ke paas ₹20 Lakhs tak ke cases the, aur DCs/ACs ₹20 Lakhs se upar ke cases dekhte the.

*   **Naye Limits (Delhi Region ke liye, immediately effective):**
    *   **Corporate Returns:** Ab ITOs ₹70 Lakhs tak ke cases handle karenge, aur ₹70 Lakhs se upar ke cases DCs/ACs ke paas jayenge.
    *   **Non-Corporate Returns (Salary Income ko chhodkar):** ITOs ₹40 Lakhs tak ke cases dekhenge, aur ₹40 Lakhs se upar ke cases DCs/ACs ko allot honge.
    *   **Non-Corporate Returns (Salary Income):** ₹30 Lakhs tak ke salary income cases ITOs ke jurisdiction mein rahenge, aur ₹30 Lakhs se upar ke cases DCs/ACs ke paas jayenge.

Yeh revised limits income aur loss dono tarah ke returns par uniformly apply honge. Is kadam ka main maksad assessment workload ko rationalize karna, cases ka proportionate allocation ensure karna, aur Delhi Region mein future PAN allocation/transfer ko streamline karna hai.

## Read the Official Document
*   [Download the Official Notification/Circular here](https://www.incometax.gov.in/)

*Published on: 16 May 2026*
